data.description_suggestion: Creates a new e-commerce order in the iFirma system. Accepts an OrderRequest containing order details including items, billing address, shipping address, customer information, and payment details. Returns an OrderResponse with the created order confirmation. Requires Bearer token authentication. The order is persisted in the system and assigned a unique order ID upon successful creation.
data.reasoning: The description is completely empty, leaving AI agents unable to understand what inputs are required, what the operation returns, or what side effects occur. Added comprehensive details about the OrderRequest schema usage, OrderResponse return value, authentication requirement, and the side effect of order persistence with ID assignment. This enables AI agents to determine when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'updateorder' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, missing_context, poor_ai_interpretability. The description is empty, preventing AI agents from understanding the operation's purpose, required inputs, expected outputs, or behavioral characteristics. Added details about what can be updated, the requirement to include order ID in the request, authentication needs, return value, and clarification that this supports partial updates. This helps AI agents distinguish between create and update operations and understand when to use each.

data.description_suggestion: Updates an existing e-commerce order in the iFirma system. Accepts an OrderRequest containing the order ID and fields to be updated, such as items, addresses, customer information, or tracking details. Returns an OrderResponse with the updated order confirmation. Requires Bearer token authentication. Modifies the order record in the system; only provided fields are updated while existing data is preserved. Partial updates are supported.
